I treated the 69 prior turns as a proposal log, not as proof. The prompt fixes the special tame-format submission with distinguished sigma,tau, implicit tau^sigma=tau^2, later pro-2-core generators, and exponents reduced modulo M=85667662080.The prior log‚Äôs only serious unrefuted candidate remains the dynamic tame-inertia word; the earlier scalar, plain-œÄ2, and fixed-œÑ‚àí2 variants were refuted there by finite tame or index-two subgroup tests.

The live candidate is still variables sigma,tau,x,y definitions nx = (x*tau^50932630783)^40491355905 ny = (y*tau)^40491355905 relations x*sigma^45176306175*x*sigma^40491355905*x^-1*nx*ny^sigma*y^-1

Equivalently, with œÄ=œÄ2‚ààZ and Œ∑=2œÄ on odd tame inertia,r=xœÉ‚àíœÄxœÉœÄx‚àí1(xœÑ‚àíŒ∑)œÄ ((yœÑ)œÄ)œÉy‚àí1.

The constants are œÄ‚â°40491355905,‚àíœÄ‚â°45176306175,‚àíŒ∑‚â°50932630783(modM).The 2-primary coordinate of ‚àíŒ∑ is irrelevant because that exponent is applied only to œÑ, whose finite tame images have odd order.I will keep the two ‚Äúreciprocal-prone‚Äù formulas as equations, not fractions. For the sign-cover norm coefficient,Da=j=0‚àëœÄ‚àí1(tŒ±a)j satisfies (1‚àítŒ±a)Da=1‚àí(tŒ±a)œÄ=1‚àít,soDa=(1‚àít)(1‚àítŒ±a)‚àí1.Here t is the deck involution of the sign double cover and œÑ‚Ü¶Œ±ÓÄ†=1 is a nontrivial odd tame-inertia character. This is the direction that makes Da radical, hence ‚àí1+D‚àíŒ∑ a unit. Thus the nontrivial odd tame-character blocks still contribute only unit Smith pivots.

For the trivial tame-inertia block, I use(1+T‚àís)X+(1+T)Y=0,s‚â°œÄ(modf),and define Ps (T) by (1+T)Ps (T)=1+T‚àís.

Then Y=Ps (T)X+YÀâ, and h(T)=1+Ps (T)+Ps(T‚àí1)has no nontrivial f-th-root zero because (1+Œ∂)h(Œ∂)=Œ∂‚àís+Œ∂s+1=Œ∂‚àís(1+Œ∂2s+1),while 2s+1 is coprime to f. With these conventions, the prior abelianization conclusions remain coherent:Ue,fab‚âÉZ2ef+1‚äïC2,(kerŒ∫)ab‚âÉZ22ef+1‚äïC4.New contribution: a coefficient-basis for primitive residual tests

Turn 69 gave a useful primitive-descent lemma: for R‚ààF2[Cd], the primitive exact-order c part of the folded residual œÅc (R)‚ààF2[Cc] vanishes iff œÅc (R) lies in the sum of inflations from the proper quotients c/‚Ñì. I sharpened this into an explicit coefficient basis that works uniformly for prime powers and nonsquarefree composites.Letc=i=1‚àèr‚Ñìiai be odd, and identify

Z/cZ‚âÉi‚àè

Z/‚ÑìiaiZ.

Write an index in the i-th factor asui+‚Ñìiai‚àí1vi,ui‚ààZ/‚Ñìiai‚àí1Z,vi‚ààZ/‚ÑìiZ.For a coefficient function r on Z/cZ, define the mixed top-digit differenceŒîu,vr=Œµ‚àà{0,1}r‚àër(u1+‚Ñì1a1‚àí1Œµ1v1,‚Ä¶,ur+‚Ñìrar‚àí1Œµrvr),where each viÓÄ†=0.Then the primitive exact-order c component vanishes if and only ifŒîu,vr=0 for every choice of u and every nonzero top-digit vector v.

Proof sketch: set Ac=F2 [Z/c]. The inflation from c/‚Ñìi is exactly the subspace of functions independent of the top digit vi. ThereforeAc/i‚àëInf c/‚ÑìicA c/‚Ñìi‚âÉi‚®Ç(F2 [Z/‚Ñìiai]/Inf‚Ñìiai‚àí1‚ÑìiaiF2 [Z/‚Ñìiai‚àí1]).A basis of the dual quotient is given by the top-digit difference functionals above. This quotient is exactly the primitive character component, of dimension œÜ(c).This recovers the earlier tests but makes the nonsquarefree composite cases explicit:

For c=‚Ñìa, primitive vanishing is block constancy:ru=r u+‚Ñìa‚àí1=‚ãØ=r u+(‚Ñì‚àí1)‚Ñìa‚àí1 (umod‚Ñì a‚àí1).

For c=pq, it is the rectangle condition:ru,v+ru‚Ä≤,v+ru,v‚Ä≤+ru‚Ä≤,v‚Ä≤=0.For c=99=9‚ãÖ11, write the 9-coordinate as a+3u, with a,u‚ààZ/3, and the 11-coordinate as v. Primitive 99-vanishing is exactlyra+3u,v+ra,v+ra+3u,0+ra,0=0 for all a, all uÓÄ†=0, and all vÓÄ†=0. This is the first place where the prime-power and squarefree tests genuinely combine.

This gives a basis-free residual protocol:Fold the normalized arithmetic-minus-candidate residual to c.Apply the mixed top-digit differences above.If all vanish, descend to the proper quotient components.If any survives, it is a genuine primitive filtered-action obstruction.So the next arithmetic computations should not use cyclotomic division or ambiguous basis choices. They should compute R(T), fold to c, and test these mixed differences.

Current priority remains:c=3,¬†5,¬†9,¬†11,¬†17,¬†13,¬†33,¬†19,¬†27,¬†25,‚Ä¶ but the new basis makes later tests such as c=99,129,145 mechanically unambiguous.

ESTABLISHED: The dynamic candidate remains the only unrefuted candidate. I preserved the stable equationsDa=(1‚àít)(1‚àítŒ±a)‚àí1,(1+T)Ps (T)=1+T‚àís.New this turn: I gave an explicit coefficient-basis for primitive exact-order residuals. For c=‚àè‚Ñìiai, primitive vanishing is equivalent to the vanishing of all mixed top-digit differences Œîu,v r. This generalizes the prime-power block-constancy and squarefree rectangle tests.

GAP: No actual arithmetic-minus-candidate residual has been computed. The presentation is therefore still not proved. The missing step remains either a dyadic formation theorem or explicit Kummer/Witt arithmetic showing that the true filtered tame action has zero residual in every primitive anti-cyclotomic component.

NEXT: Compute normalized arithmetic residuals first for c=3,5,9,11. For c=9, apply top-digit block constancy and descend to c=3. For c=33,57,65, apply rectangle tests. For c=99 and other nonsquarefree composites, use the mixed top-digit differences above.[BREAKTHROUGH]
